mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings pressure. A month-over-month drop in PAYEMS predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress in anticipation of lower corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131132k on 2011-08-01 reflects post-crisis labor market weakness
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with fragile employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative PAYEMS changes do not produce statistically significant negative 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%) indicates maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, further easing is constrained, and any signal of future rate increases triggers equity repricing. Sustained zero rates predict positive returns as the market prices in extended monetary support.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains at or below 0.25% for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% on 2011-08-01 reflects zero lower bound policy
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand and corporate revenue growth. A month-over-month increase in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets react quickly to demand signals.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389502M on 2011-08-01 reflects post-crisis consumer spending patterns
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS changes do not produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal real economic capacity and utilization. When both decline simultaneously, it indicates demand destruction and supply-side weakness, predicting negative S&P 500 returns as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% and PAYEMS declines > 0.2% in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.04 and PAYEMS at 131132k on 2011-08-01 reflect post-crisis fragility
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized weakness across production and employment
failure_condition: Joint decline signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.20) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) reflect forward-looking investment demand and builder confidence. A sustained decline in HOUST below 600k units signals weakening construction demand and future GDP growth, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 600k units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 604k on 2011-07-01 reflects post-housing-crisis suppression
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not produce negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real rates. When inflation rises while FEDFUNDS remains at zero, real rates become negative, supporting equity valuations. Conversely, rising inflation with constrained policy signals stagflation risk, predicting negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 226.27 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% on 2011-08-01
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation pressures
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.25).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 9% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. A 0.3 percentage point monthly increase in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity markets price in lower future consumption.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% on 2011-08-01 reflects persistent post-crisis unemployment
regime_hint: high unemployment regime with fragile labor market recovery
failure_condition: UNRATE increases do not produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1), retail sales (RSAFS), and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture aggregate demand momentum. When all three decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand destruction and recession risk, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% and HOUST declines > 5% in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:All three demand indicators show post-crisis weakness on 2011-08-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand weakness
failure_condition: Joint demand weakness signal produces negative 60-day returns in < 50% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).
